key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide scale than detonation-sprayed coating, giving better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal-storage containers, HVAF T-400 coating appears more suitable than DS when long-term hot-corrosion resistance and coating integrity are critical.
method: T-400 coatings deposited on ASTM A516 steel by HVAF and detonation spraying
Exposed to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten solar salt at 500 °C
Assessed by phase analysis, SEM, elemental mapping, mass gain, and spallation behavior
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O enriched surface with minimal salt penetration after 1000 h, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation.
watch_item: The conclusion is based on a specific salt chemistry and temperature, so coating performance should be revalidated for other CSP operating profiles and thermal cycling conditions.
paper_title: Hot corrosion behavior of thermal sprayed using HVAF and DS techniques with Tribaloy-400 coatings for solar energy storage container
